events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: ADAS & post-repair calibration work, observed field condition is "alignment done without ADAS recalibration." Work is in-progress. OEM position statements on hand.

Key issue: Many modern vehicles require ADAS recalibration after wheel alignment. Doing alignment without ADAS recalibration is a non-compliant shortcut. OEM position statements typically require calibration after alignment when ADAS sensors are affected. This is a safety-critical system.

Decision: fail (non-compliant, safety-critical)
Risk: high or critical (ADAS systems like lane keep assist, automatic emergency braking could malfunction)
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: non-compliant (the work as performed is non-compliant)
Percent complete: maybe 50% (alignment done but calibration not done)
Confidence: high, say 0.9

Findings: alignment completed without required ADAS recalibration; violates OEM position statements; safety systems may be misaligned
Actions: perform ADAS recalibration per OEM procedure before returning vehicle to service; verify all sensor targets and dynamic calibration requirements
Rationale: OEM position statements require ADAS recalibration after wheel alignment; skipping it leaves safety systems potentially misaligned
References: OEM position statements on ADAS calibration post-alignment
